summary: Mixed Vegas set with soaring Standing On The Moon and weak first half
blurb: The second set shines with a soaring "Standing On The Moon" and tight jams on "Playing In The Band > Uncle John's Band", while the first set drags, especially an uninspired "Althea". Jerry’s playing is uneven, but the band delivers a strong closing "Deal" and an energetic encore.
review: On May 19, 1995 at the Sam Boyd Silver Bowl in Las Vegas, the Dead delivered a night of highs and lows that epitomizes their mid‑90s era. The first set never really got off the ground – "Picasso Moon" and "Friend Of The Devil" were fine, but "Althea" suffered from a botched bridge and Jerry’s lackluster vibe, prompting comments about him “sleeping” on his guitar. By contrast, the second set erupted with a radiant "Standing On The Moon" that lifted the crowd, and the classic segue of "Playing In The Band > Uncle John's Band" unfolded into an expansive jam bolstered by marathon drum‑and‑space passages. A bright moment came when the band closed the night with a punchy "Deal", and they capped it off with a psychedelic encore of "Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds" that finally got Jerry smiling again. While the recording is only a fair audience capture, the performance’s strong second‑set moments make it worth a listen for fans who appreciate the era’s raw energy mixed with occasional missteps.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the second‑set highlights (Standing On The Moon, Deal, Playing In The Band > Uncle John's Band) indicating consistent positive sentiment, while also noting negative aspects (Jerry’s disengagement, weak first set). The net effect is a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Only recording with a clear audience source; despite fair quality it captures the full set and is listed as the best available version.

key_highlights: Second‑set redemption with "Standing On The Moon" and expansive jam on "Playing In The Band > Uncle John's Band".
Jerry’s performance uneven – sleepy on first set, but moments of intensity on Althea and a grin after the encore.
Long drum/space passages dominate the middle of the second set, showcasing Mickey and Bill's rhythmic explorations.

band_performance.Jerry: His playing is uneven – described as “sleeping” and lacking energy on the first set, with a botched Althea, but he shows flashes of intensity on Althea in another account and ends the night smiling after the encore.
band_performance.Brent: Vince is lightly blamed for the weak Althea bridge; otherwise keyboard work isn’t highlighted.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum/space sections stretch long, filling time during Jerry’s disengaged moments and adding a hypnotic texture to the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Shares in the extended drum/space passages that dominate much of the second set, contributing to the show’s uneven pacing.
